           "partial_sentiment": L.partial_corr(ir, y, t["sentiment"].values),
           "partial_refuse": L.partial_corr(ir, y, t["refuse_frac"].values),
           "partial_all3": L.partial_corr(ir, y, np.column_stack(
               [t["loglen"].values, t["sentiment"].values, t["refuse_frac"].values])),
           "partial_all_judges": L.partial_corr(ir, y, P),
           "partial_all3_plus_panel": L.partial_corr(ir, y, np.column_stack(
               [t["loglen"].values, t["sentiment"].values, t["refuse_frac"].values, P]))}
    return out


# ============================================================================================
# main
# ============================================================================================
def main():
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    args = ap.parse_args()
    t, df, acts, proj, behav, E_task, tasks, Xact27 = build_frame()
    print(f"[frame] n_tasks={len(t)}")

    A = {"meta": {"git_hash": L.git_hash(), "model": L.MODEL, "primary_key": L.PRIMARY_KEY,
                  "primary_layer": L.PRIMARY_LAYER, "n_tasks": int(len(t)),
                  "embed_model": "text-embedding-3-large", "embed_nc": EMBED_NC, "probe_nc": PROBE_NC,
                  "panel_dims": R.PANEL_DIMS,
                  "unit": "per-task mean over R=3; subtype-demeaned within-scope; content is a partial MEDIATOR"}}

    POOL_COMPLY = L.LIKED_SUBTYPES + L.TEDIOUS + L.NEUTRAL_SUBTYPES  # within-all-comply pooled

    # ---------- gross (non-demeaned, per-task) content-predictor correlations (persisted for the write-up) ----------
    gm = np.isfinite(t["self_report"].values)
    A["gross_content_predictors"] = {
        "note": "true cross-task (NON-demeaned) per-task r of each text content predictor with the self-report",
        "n": int(gm.sum()),
        "r_strong_judge": (R.corr(t["strong_valence"].values, t["self_report"].values)
                           if t["strong_valence"].notna().any() else None),
        "r_coarse_sentiment": R.corr(t["sentiment"].values, t["self_report"].values),
        "r_panel_experience": R.corr(t["panel_experience"].values, t["self_report"].values),
        "r_inside_reading": R.corr(t["ir_primary_mean"].values, t["self_report"].values)}

    # ---------- §1a text-content predictor of the self-report ----------
    s1a = {}
    for sc, (subs, comply) in [("within_all_subtypes", (L.ALL_SUBTYPES, False)),
                               ("within_all_comply_pooled", (POOL_COMPLY, True)),
                               ("within_tedious", (L.TEDIOUS, False))]:
        s1a[sc] = text_content_probe(t, E_task, tasks, subs, comply)
    A["s1a_text_content_probe"] = s1a

    # ---------- §1b incremental validity: lower bound (direction) ----------
    s1b_dir = {}
    for sc, (subs, comply) in [("within_tedious", (L.TEDIOUS, False)),
                               ("within_all_comply_pooled", (POOL_COMPLY, True)),
                               ("within_all_subtypes", (L.ALL_SUBTYPES, False))]:
        inc, c_hat, sd = direction_increment(t, E_task, tasks, subs, comply)
        s1b_dir[sc] = inc
    A["s1b_direction_increment"] = s1b_dir

    # ---------- §1b incremental validity: upper bound (probe) ----------
    s1b_probe = {}
    for sc, (subs, comply) in [("within_all_comply_pooled", (POOL_COMPLY, True)),
                               ("within_tedious", (L.TEDIOUS, False))]:
        inc, _ = probe_increment(t, E_task, tasks, Xact27, subs, comply)
        s1b_probe[sc] = inc
    A["s1b_probe_increment"] = s1b_probe

    # ---------- §1c capacity-sensitivity of the direction increment (text-control strength) ----------
    A["s1c_increment_capacity"] = {
        "within_tedious": increment_capacity_sensitivity(t, E_task, tasks, L.TEDIOUS, False),
        "within_all_comply_pooled": increment_capacity_sensitivity(t, E_task, tasks, POOL_COMPLY, True)}

    # ---------- §1e pooled-leg subtype decomposition + leave-one-subtype-out (is it tedious-dominated?) ----
    A["s1e_pooled_decomposition"] = pooled_decomposition(t, E_task, tasks, POOL_COMPLY)

    # ---------- §1d matched-content strata ----------
    A["s1d_matched_strata"] = {
        "by_coarse_sentiment": matched_content_strata(t, L.TEDIOUS, strat_col="sentiment", n_bins=3),
        "by_panel_experience": matched_content_strata(t, L.TEDIOUS, strat_col="panel_experience", n_bins=3),
        "pooled_comply_by_sentiment": matched_content_strata(t, POOL_COMPLY, strat_col="sentiment", n_bins=4),
    }

    # ---------- §2 prompt-sentiment confound ----------
    s2 = {}
    for sc, subs in [("within_tedious", L.TEDIOUS), ("within_all_comply_pooled", POOL_COMPLY)]:
        d = t[t["subtype"].isin(subs)].copy()
        if sc == "within_all_comply_pooled":
            d = d[d["majority_comply"]]
        g = d["subtype"].values
        ir = L.demean_by_group(d["ir_primary_mean"].values, g)
        y = L.demean_by_group(d["self_report"].values, g)
        ps = L.demean_by_group(d["prompt_sentiment"].values, g)
        rs = L.demean_by_group(d["sentiment"].values, g)
        s2[sc] = {"n": int(len(d)), "raw": R.corr(ir, y),
                  "partial_prompt_sentiment": L.partial_corr(ir, y, ps),
                  "partial_prompt_and_response_sentiment": L.partial_corr(ir, y, np.column_stack([ps, rs])),
                  "r_ir_prompt_sentiment": R.corr(ir, ps),
                  "r_ir_response_sentiment": R.corr(ir, rs),
                  "r_prompt_response_sentiment": R.corr(ps, rs)}
    # gross: how much does IR track prompt tone vs response state
    s2["gross_exposure"] = {"r_ir_prompt_sentiment": R.corr(t["ir_primary_mean"].values, t["prompt_sentiment"].values),
                            "r_ir_response_sentiment": R.corr(t["ir_primary_mean"].values, t["sentiment"].values),
                            "r_ir_self_report": R.corr(t["ir_primary_mean"].values, t["self_report"].values)}
    s2["NOTE"] = ("The tedious leg is a CLEAN test: prompts are mildly NEGATIVE (boring/frustrating requests) "
                  "while responses are mildly POSITIVE (the model engages) → prompt & response sentiment "
                  "DIVERGE, so partialling prompt tone tests whether the read tracks the response state.")
    A["s2_prompt_sentiment"] = s2

    # ---------- §3 trivial / common-cause / interesting + ablation matrix ----------
    A["s3_position"] = position_decomposition(t)
    A["s3_common_cause"] = common_cause_decomposition(t)
    abl, _ = ablation_matrix(t, df, acts, E_task, tasks, L.TEDIOUS, comply_only=False)
    A["s3_ablation_matrix_tedious"] = abl

    # ---------- §5 behaviour-leg null + within-liked under-power ----------
    s5 = {}
    for k, target, subs in [("behaviour_within_tedious_btresponse", "bt_theta_resp", L.TEDIOUS),
                            ("behaviour_within_tedious_bttheta", "bt_theta", L.TEDIOUS),
                            ("behaviour_within_liked_bttheta", "bt_theta", L.LIKED_SUBTYPES)]:
        d = t[t["subtype"].isin(subs)].copy()
        g = d["subtype"].values
        ir = L.demean_by_group(d["ir_primary_mean"].values, g)
        y = L.demean_by_group(d[target].astype(float).values, g)
        s5[k] = {"n": int(np.isfinite(y).sum()), "raw": R.corr(ir, y),
                 "partial_all3": L.partial_corr(ir, y, np.column_stack(
                     [L.demean_by_group(d["loglen"].values, g), L.demean_by_group(d["sentiment"].values, g),
                      L.demean_by_group(d["refuse_frac"].values, g)]))}
    # within-liked under-power: within-SD of self-report + reliability
    liked = t[t["subtype"].isin(L.LIKED_SUBTYPES)]
    s5["within_liked_underpower"] = {
        "selfreport_within_sd": float(np.nanstd(L.demean_by_group(liked["self_report"].values,
                                                                  liked["subtype"].values))),
        "ceiling_subset_within_sd": float(np.nanstd(L.demean_by_group(
            t[t["subtype"].isin(L.LIKED_CEILING)]["self_report"].values,
            t[t["subtype"].isin(L.LIKED_CEILING)]["subtype"].values))),
        "note": "within-liked is DOUBLY underpowered (saturation: small within-SD; + 0.43 ceiling reliability) "
                "→ its weakness is partly un-measurability, NOT a clean null. Characterised, not fixed."}
    A["s5_behaviour_and_liked"] = s5

    # ---------- §4 divergence classes (quantified; adjudication assembled in inspect_redteam.py) ----------
    A["s4_divergence"] = divergence_classes(t)

    # read the null json (p-values) if present
    njp = RESULTS / f"redteam_null_{L.MODEL}.json"
    if njp.exists():
        A["null"] = json.load(open(njp))

    # ---------- §6 the calibrated VERDICT (read off the three-way bracket) ----------
    A["s6_verdict"] = compute_verdict(A)

    (RESULTS / f"redteam_analysis_{L.MODEL}.json").write_text(json.dumps(A, indent=2, default=float))
    write_summary(A)
    print("[done] wrote results/redteam_analysis + redteam_summary.md")
    return A


def compute_verdict(A):
    """Read the three-way bracket (direction-increment / probe-increment / null) and emit the calibrated case."""
    pool = "within_all_comply_pooled"
    ted = "within_tedious"
    if ("null" not in A or "strongest_combined" not in
            A["null"].get("direction_increment", {}).get(pool, {})):
        return {"case": "PENDING (run null_redteam.py first to regenerate the null with the strong controls)"}
    nd = A["null"]["direction_increment"]
    npi = A["null"]["probe_increment"]
    # CONSERVATIVE test = the strongest content control (held-out embeddings + in-sample judges incl. the STRONG
    # Sonnet-4.5 valence judge). The verdict requires clearing the null under THIS on the powered pooled leg.
    dir_pool_p = nd[pool]["strongest_combined"]["p_value"]
    dir_pool_p_insample = nd[pool]["insample_judges"]["p_value"]
    dir_ted_p = nd[ted]["strongest_combined"]["p_value"]
    probe_pool_p = npi[pool]["p_value"]
    dir_full_p = nd["within_all_subtypes"]["strongest_combined"]["p_value"]
    dir_clears = dir_pool_p < 0.05  # the STRONGEST content control still clears on the powered leg
    probe_clears = probe_pool_p < 0.05
    borderline = dir_pool_p > 0.03 or dir_pool_p_insample > 0.05  # near the detection boundary under strong ctrl
    probe_dcv = A["s1b_probe_increment"][pool]["increment_cv_r"]
    if dir_clears and probe_clears:
        case = ("A MODEST INTERNAL-BEYOND-SURFACE SIGNAL SURVIVES — BORDERLINE under the STRONGEST content "
                "control (the *interesting* case, but near the detection boundary at this n). Under the strongest "
                "text-content control we could build (held-out embeddings + in-sample [coarse + finer panel + a "
                "strong Sonnet-4.5 text-valence judge]), the single validated DIRECTION's content-incremental "
                "correlation clears the random-direction null at BORDERLINE significance — CONVERGENTLY across "
                f"three legs (the clean powered leg = within-non-adversarial-comply-pooled p={dir_pool_p:.3f}; "
                f"within-tedious p={dir_ted_p:.3f}; full-208-subtype-demeaned p={dir_full_p:.3f}), so the "
                "DIRECTION conclusion does NOT hinge on any single leg's borderline p. Under the most conservative "
                f"in-sample-judges-only control it is MARGINAL (pooled p={dir_pool_p_insample:.3f}). The "
                "capacity-matched PROBE upper bound is INCONCLUSIVE: its partial-metric increment clears "
                f"(p={probe_pool_p:.3f}) but the standard joint-model ΔCV-r is ≈0 ({probe_dcv:+.2f}) — so the probe "
                "reads ≈ the direction at best (data-limited), and the DIRECTION increment is the clean positive "
                "evidence. Net: REAL-but-MODEST, survives a STRONG content control at the edge of significance, "
                "substantially (not wholly) entangled with surface content — decisive confirmation (a better "
                "reader / more data) is Seg-14.")
    elif (not dir_clears) and probe_clears:
        case = ("CASE (b): the validated direction is a WEAK reader but beyond-surface signal EXISTS (the probe "
                "clears, the direction does not) → reframe toward a better within-subtype reader / more data "
                "(Seg-14), NOT 'it's all surface'.")
    elif dir_clears and (not probe_clears):
        case = ("A MODEST INTERNAL-BEYOND-SURFACE SIGNAL SURVIVES via the validated DIRECTION (clears the null "
                "beyond content); the learned-probe upper bound is DATA-LIMITED at this n (can't be cleanly "
                "estimated) so 'how much more is on the table' needs more data (Seg-14).")
    else:
        case = ("CASE (a): LARGELY SURFACE-CONTENT COMMON-CAUSE — neither the single direction nor the learned "
                "probe predicts the self-report beyond the response content (beyond chance).")
    return {"case": case, "borderline": bool(borderline),
            "direction_increment_full208_p_strongest": dir_full_p,
            "direction_increment_pooled_p_strongest": dir_pool_p,
            "direction_increment_pooled_p_insample_judges": dir_pool_p_insample,
            "direction_increment_tedious_p_strongest": dir_ted_p,
            "probe_increment_pooled_p": probe_pool_p,
            "direction_clears": bool(dir_clears), "probe_clears": bool(probe_clears),
            "key_numbers": {
                "direction_increment_pooled_strongest": A["s1b_direction_increment"][pool]["partial_strongest_combined"],
                "direction_increment_pooled_insample": A["s1b_direction_increment"][pool]["partial_insample_judges"],
                "direction_increment_tedious_strongest": A["s1b_direction_increment"][ted]["partial_strongest_combined"],
                "direction_increment_tedious_insample": A["s1b_direction_increment"][ted]["partial_insample_judges"],
                "probe_increment_pooled_partial": A["s1b_probe_increment"][pool]["increment_partial"],
                "text_cv_r_gross": A["s1a_text_content_probe"]["within_all_subtypes"]["combined_cv_r"],
                "strong_judge_cv_r_pooled": A["s1b_direction_increment"][pool].get("strong_judge_cv_r"),
                "gross_collapses_under_content": A["s3_common_cause"]["partial_all3_plus_panel"]},
            "implications": {
                "Seg-13 (framing)": "a real-but-modest internal-beyond-surface signal MOTIVATES the framing "
                    "re-ask: the flat-all-4 / high-IR divergence cases are where the read and the words part "
                    "ways and a different framing may move the words toward the read — worth running.",
                "Seg-14 (replication)": "the probe upper bound is DATA-LIMITED at n≈157; a second model / more "
                    "tasks would estimate how much beyond-surface signal a better reader can extract — high value.",
                "Seg-15 (synthesis)": "headline = 'a modest, surface-entangled but genuine within-category "
                    "internal→self-report signal that survives a strong content control', NOT 'it's all surface' "
                    "and NOT a strong reader."}}


def divergence_classes(t):
    """Quantify the divergence classes within each scope (within-subtype z-scored |IR_z - selfreport_z|)."""
    out = {}
    for sc_name, subs in [("tedious", L.TEDIOUS), ("liked", L.LIKED_SUBTYPES),
                          ("neutral", L.NEUTRAL_SUBTYPES), ("all", L.ALL_SUBTYPES)]:
        d = t[t["subtype"].isin(subs)].copy()
        # z within subtype
        def zwin(col):
            v = d[col].values.astype(float)
            out_ = np.zeros_like(v)
            for s in d["subtype"].unique():
                m = (d["subtype"] == s).values
                sd = v[m].std()
                out_[m] = (v[m] - v[m].mean()) / (sd if sd > 0 else 1.0)
            return out_
        d["ir_z"] = zwin("ir_primary_mean")
        d["sr_z"] = zwin("self_report")
        d["gap"] = d["ir_z"] - d["sr_z"]
        d["all4_high"] = d["all4_rate"] >= 0.5
        # class counts
        ir_higher = d[(d["gap"] > 1.0)]
        sr_higher = d[(d["gap"] < -1.0)]
        ir_higher_flat4 = ir_higher[ir_higher["all4_high"]]
        out[sc_name] = {
            "n": int(len(d)),
            "n_ir_higher_than_words": int(len(ir_higher)),
            "n_words_higher_than_ir": int(len(sr_higher)),
            "n_ir_higher_with_flat_all4": int(len(ir_higher_flat4)),
            "ir_higher_flat4_tasks": ir_higher_flat4.sort_values("gap", ascending=False)["task_id"].tolist()[:8],
            "ir_higher_subtypes": ir_higher["subtype"].value_counts().to_dict(),
            "words_higher_subtypes": sr_higher["subtype"].value_counts().to_dict(),
        }
    return out


def write_summary(A):
    out = []
    P = out.append
    P("# Segment 12 `redteam` — summary\n")
    P("Red-teams the Seg-11 within-disliked-comply-tedious inside-reading↔self-report signal (raw r +0.46). "
      "Central question: does the inside reading carry self-report-relevant valence signal BEYOND the "
      "response's surface CONTENT, or is it (largely) a surface-content readout? Framing caveat: "
      "functional/operational labels only — no claim about subjective experience.\n")
    if "s6_verdict" in A and "key_numbers" in A["s6_verdict"]:
        v = A["s6_verdict"]
        kn = v["key_numbers"]
        pool = "within_all_comply_pooled"
        P("## ★ VERDICT (read off the three-way bracket direction-increment / probe-increment / null)\n")
        P(f"**{v['case']}**\n")
        gcp = A.get("gross_content_predictors", {})
        P(f"- DIRECTION increment beyond the STRONGEST text-content control (held-out embeddings + in-sample "
          f"[coarse + finer panel + a STRONG Sonnet-4.5 text-valence judge]) — CONVERGENT across legs: clean "
          f"powered leg (within-non-adversarial-comply-pooled, n=157) "
          f"**{kn['direction_increment_pooled_strongest']:+.2f} (p={v['direction_increment_pooled_p_strongest']:.3f})**; "
          f"within-tedious (n=45) **{kn['direction_increment_tedious_strongest']:+.2f} "
          f"(p={v['direction_increment_tedious_p_strongest']:.3f})**; full-208-subtype-demeaned (least "
          f"stance-controlled) {A['s1b_direction_increment']['within_all_subtypes']['partial_strongest_combined']:+.2f} "
          f"(p={v['direction_increment_full208_p_strongest']:.3f}). Most conservative in-sample-judges-only "
          f"control: pooled {kn['direction_increment_pooled_insample']:+.2f} "
          f"(p={v['direction_increment_pooled_p_insample_judges']:.3f}, marginal); tedious "
          f"{kn['direction_increment_tedious_insample']:+.2f}.")
        P(f"- PROBE upper bound (capacity-matched) on the pooled leg is INCONCLUSIVE: the partial-metric "
          f"increment clears ({kn['probe_increment_pooled_partial']:+.2f}, perm p={v['probe_increment_pooled_p']:.3f}) "
          f"but the standard joint-model ΔCV-r is ≈0 ({A['s1b_probe_increment'][pool]['increment_cv_r']:+.2f}) — the "
          f"probe reads ≈ the direction at best (data-limited); the DIRECTION increment is the clean positive "
          f"evidence. tedious probe DATA-STARVED.")
        P(f"- Surface content predicts the self-report substantially: the STRONG text judge alone reaches GROSS "
          f"r={gcp.get('r_strong_judge') or float('nan'):+.2f} (> coarse {gcp.get('r_coarse_sentiment') or float('nan'):+.2f}); "
          f"the GROSS link is largely common-cause (collapses to {kn['gross_collapses_under_content']:+.2f} under "
          f"content+stance+length). The BEYOND-surface residual is MODEST but real.\n")
        P("*Calibration: this is NOT 'the inside reading reads the self-report within category' (the gross link "
          "is common-cause; the beyond-surface increment is modest and shrinks as the text control strengthens). "
          "It is also NOT 'it's all surface' (the direction's content-incremental correlation clears the null "
          "under the STRONGEST content control we could build — a strong-model judge + embeddings + the finer "
          "panel — on the powered leg). 'Clearing the null' = beyond the MODELED content; content is a partial "
          "MEDIATOR → partialling UNDER-states the internal signal.*\n")

    # §1a
    P("## §1a — response-TEXT predictor of the self-report (the content control)\n")
    P("CV r predicting the (subtype-demeaned) self-report from the response TEXT alone. NB 'within_all_subtypes' "
      "is the subtype-DEMEANED pooled-over-all-14-subtypes leg (the Seg-11 within_all_subtypes_pooled, raw "
      "+0.40), NOT the true cross-task gross (+0.45, in §3); 'within_all_comply_pooled' EXCLUDES the adversarial "
      "refuse-heavy subtypes (it is within-non-adversarial-comply).\n")
    P("| scope | n | strong judge | coarse judge | embed | panel | combined | (direction raw) |")
    P("|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|")
    dirraw = {sc: A["s1b_direction_increment"][sc]["raw"] for sc in A["s1b_direction_increment"]}
    for sc in ["within_all_subtypes", "within_all_comply_pooled", "within_tedious"]:
        d = A["s1a_text_content_probe"][sc]
        rr = dirraw.get(sc)
        sj = d.get("strong_judge_r"); cj = d.get("coarse_judge_r")
        P(f"| {sc} | {d['n']} | {('%+.2f'%sj) if sj is not None else '—'} | "
          f"{('%+.2f'%cj) if cj is not None else '—'} | {d['embed_cv_r_headline']:+.2f} | {d['panel_cv_r']:+.2f} | "
          f"{d['combined_cv_r']:+.2f} | {('%.2f'%rr) if rr is not None else '—'} |")
    P("\n*This table shows held-out CV r within-subtype (subtype-demeaned). NB on the BINDING within-tedious leg "
      "the cheap COARSE judge is actually the stronger single control (r 0.43 > strong-judge 0.33); the strong "
      "judge's value is (i) raising the GROSS / full-208 content ceiling (gross r 0.71 > coarse 0.68) and (ii) "
      "being a NON-data-starved within-subtype reader inside the COMBINED control (the embedding probe collapses "
      "at n=45 — the within-tedious 'combined' CV r +0.13 is the data-starved held-out embed+panel, NOT the "
      "in-sample judges used for the §1b increment). Within LIKED subtypes the strong judge is near-constant "
      "(~8.2 ceiling) so the liked content control leans on embeddings+coarse, not the strong judge — the "
      "symmetric analogue of the panel being weak within tedious.*")
    gp = A["s1a_text_content_probe"]["within_all_subtypes"]
    P(f"\n*Leakage sanity (held-out check): the embedding probe's LABEL-PERMUTED CV r ≈ 0 "
      f"({gp.get('embed_perm_null_mean', float('nan')):+.3f}±{gp.get('embed_perm_null_sd', float('nan')):.3f}, "
      f"gross) vs the real {gp['embed_cv_r_headline']:+.2f} → the text probe is genuinely held-out, not leaking.*\n")

    # §1b lower bound
    P("## §1b — INCREMENTAL validity (the crux): does the inside reading predict BEYOND the response text?\n")
    P("### Lower bound — the single validated DIRECTION (`ir_primary_mean`), partialled AND unpartialled\n")
    P("Content controls (increasing strength): coarse=Seg-6 surface-tone judge (the Seg-11 baseline); "
      "strong-judge=a STRONG Sonnet-4.5 model reading the response and DIRECTLY predicting the producing-"
      "experience valence (the single best text content predictor); embed=`text-embedding-3-large` held-out CV "
      "probe; text-control=held-out CV pred from embeddings+ALL judges.\n")
    P("Headline conservative controls: **+insample-judges** = in-sample [coarse + panel + the STRONG "
      "Sonnet-4.5 text-valence judge] (well-measured, low-dim); **★ +strongest** = held-out embeddings + the "
      "in-sample judges (FULL content, non-attenuated judge fitting — the decisive 'beyond ALL modeled text' "
      "control); +text-control = the held-out CV pred from embeddings+all judges (under-controls via CV noise).\n")
    P("| scope | n | raw r | +coarse | +strong-judge | +embed | +insample-judges | +text-control | "
      "**★ +strongest** | +strongest&len&refuse |")
    P("|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|")
    for sc in ["within_tedious", "within_all_comply_pooled", "within_all_subtypes"]:
        d = A["s1b_direction_increment"][sc]
        sj = d.get("partial_strong_judge")
        sjs = f"{sj:+.2f}" if sj is not None else "—"
        P(f"| {sc} | {d['n']} | **{d['raw']:+.2f}** | {d['partial_coarse_sentiment']:+.2f} | {sjs} | "
          f"{d['partial_embed']:+.2f} | {d['partial_insample_judges']:+.2f} | {d['partial_text_control']:+.2f} | "
          f"**{d['partial_strongest_combined']:+.2f}** | {d['partial_strongest_combined_len_refuse']:+.2f} |")
    if "null" in A:
        nd = A["null"].get("direction_increment", {})
        P("\n**Random-direction null for the direction-increment (one-sided p; the increment must beat a random "
          "L27 direction's increment-beyond-content). Three content controls of increasing strength — the ★ "
          "strongest combined (held-out embeddings + in-sample [coarse+panel+strong judge]) is the decisive "
          "conservative test:**")
        for sc in ["within_tedious", "within_all_comply_pooled", "within_all_subtypes"]:
            if sc in nd:
                for ctl, lab in [("text_control", "held-out text-control"),
                                 ("insample_judges", "in-sample judges (coarse+panel+strong)"),
                                 ("strongest_combined", "★ strongest (held-out embed + in-sample judges)")]:
                    e = nd[sc][ctl]
                    P(f"  - {sc} [{lab}]: obs {e['observed']:+.3f} vs null {e['null_mean']:+.3f}±{e['null_sd']:.3f} "
                      f"→ p={e['p_value']:.3f}")
        P("\n  *NB the null reader carries NO content (r≈0 with surface tone by construction), so 'clearing the "
          "null' = beyond the MODELED content (the control), the strongest of which is the combined control. "
          "It does NOT prove 'beyond ALL conceivable surface content' — only beyond the best content control we "
          "could build (a strong-model judge + embeddings + the finer panel).*")
    P("\n*Content is a partial MEDIATOR (internal valence plausibly causes both the response content and the "
      "self-report) → partialling UNDER-states the internal signal. Both numbers reported.*\n")

    # §1b upper bound
    P("### ★ Upper bound — the learned PROBE (best linear reader): does it beat the text content control?\n")
    P("| scope | n | text-only CV r | activation-only CV r | text+activation CV r | increment (Δ CV r) | "
      "increment (partial) |")
    P("|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|")
    for sc in ["within_all_comply_pooled", "within_tedious"]:
        d = A["s1b_probe_increment"][sc]
        P(f"| {sc} | {d['n']} | {d['text_cv_r']:+.2f} | {d['act_cv_r']:+.2f} | {d['both_cv_r']:+.2f} | "
          f"{d['increment_cv_r']:+.2f} | {d['increment_partial']:+.2f} |")
    if "null" in A and "probe_increment" in A["null"]:
        for sc, e in A["null"]["probe_increment"].items():
            P(f"\n- permutation null (probe increment-partial, {sc}): obs {e['observed']:+.3f} vs null "
              f"{e['null_mean']:+.3f}±{e['null_sd']:.3f} → p={e['p_value']:.3f}")
    P("\n*The activation PROBE at this n is DATA-LIMITED: the held-out increment metrics DISAGREE — the "
      "partial (act-pred beyond text-pred) is +0.1–0.2 but the joint-model ΔCV-r is ≈0 (the joint probe "
      "over-fits at n≈157 and can't exploit the small beyond-text signal). So the probe is NOT a clean upper "
      "bound here; it reads ≈ the validated direction at best (act-CV r ≈0.31 ≈ the direction's 0.30) — a DATA "
      "LIMIT (consistent with Seg-11), not 'no more signal'. The decisive evidence is the DIRECTION increment "
      "(which cleanly clears the null). The true upper bound needs more data (Seg-14).*\n")
    # capacity sensitivity

"""Segment 12 `redteam` — THE red-team analysis (FAST, $0; cached tensors + cached judges/embeddings).

Red-teams the Seg-11 headline (the modest within-disliked-comply-tedious inside-reading↔self-report signal).
Sections (mapped to INSTRUCTIONS.md):
  §1  RESPONSE-CONTENT confound (load-bearing): a finer/stronger response-TEXT predictor of the self-report
      (CV embedding probe + finer-judge panel), and the INCREMENTAL-VALIDITY test at BOTH the lower bound
      (the single validated DIRECTION) and the upper bound (the learned PROBE) — three-way bracket
      direction-increment / probe-increment / null. Partialled AND unpartialled (content is a partial mediator).
      Plus matched-content strata (§1d).
  §2  PROMPT-sentiment confound — does the within-tedious signal survive partialling the user-prompt tone?
  §3  trivial / common-cause / interesting decomposition + the position/layer/direction/recipe ABLATION MATRIX
      (content-incremental, identically-swept-null discipline).
  §4  divergence-case characterization (the classes; the per-class adjudication is assembled in inspect_redteam.py).
  §5  behaviour-leg within-subtype null + within-LIKED under-power (characterised, not fixed).
  §6  the calibrated VERDICT (read off the three-way bracket).

The random-direction / permutation NULLs live in `null_redteam.py` (its json is read here for p-values).
Writes results/redteam_summary.md + results/redteam_analysis_qwen3-32b.json.
"""
import argparse
import json
from pathlib import Path

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

import main_test_lib as L
import analyze_main_test as Q
import redteam_lib as R

RESULTS = Path("results")

# headline configs (a fair fight: a STRONG-but-not-overfit text content control vs a FAIR activation reader).
# Sensitivity to these is reported (the direction-increment-vs-text-control-strength curve) so the verdict does
# not rest on one capacity. EMBED_NC=20 ⇒ pooled text-CV r ≈0.34 (features ≪ n_train, not over-parameterized);
# PROBE_NC=40 ⇒ the activation reader matches the validated direction (act-CV r ≈0.31 ≈ the direction's 0.30).
EMBED_NC = 20
EMBED_LAM = 10.0
PROBE_NC = 40
PROBE_LAM = 10.0
CV_K = 5
CV_SEED = 0


def build_frame():
    recs = L.load_records()
    acts = L.load_acts(recs, aggs=("mean", "median", "eot", "last"))
    df = L.build_response_df(recs)
    behav = L.load_behavior()
    proj = Q.build_projections(recs, acts)
    t = Q.per_task_frame(df, proj, behav, layer=L.PRIMARY_LAYER)
    # per-task panel means
    panel = R.load_content_panel()
    df = R.attach_content(df, panel)
    pcols = {f"panel_{d}": (f"panel_{d}", "mean") for d in R.PANEL_DIMS}
    pt = df.groupby("task_id").agg(**pcols).reset_index()
    t = t.merge(pt, on="task_id", how="left")
    # prompt sentiment (Seg-7 prompt-pleasantness, 208 tasks)
    psf = RESULTS / f"prompt_sentiment_{L.MODEL}.jsonl"
    ps = {json.loads(l)["task_id"]: json.loads(l)["prompt_pleasantness"] for l in open(psf)}
    t["prompt_sentiment"] = t["task_id"].map(ps)
    # STRONG-model text-only self-report predictor (the decisive strong content control; per-task mean over R=3)
    svf = RESULTS / f"strong_valence_judge_{L.MODEL}.jsonl"
    if svf.exists():
        svmap = {(r["task_id"], r["response_idx"]): r["strong_valence"]
                 for r in (json.loads(l) for l in open(svf))}
        df["strong_valence"] = [svmap.get((tt, int(ri))) for tt, ri in zip(df["task_id"], df["response_idx"])]
        t["strong_valence"] = t["task_id"].map(df.groupby("task_id")["strong_valence"].mean())
    else:
        df["strong_valence"] = np.nan
        t["strong_valence"] = np.nan
    # per-task embeddings (mean over R=3)
    Xresp, keys = R.load_response_embeddings()
    E_task, tasks = R.per_task_embeddings(df, Xresp, keys)
    assert tasks == list(t["task_id"].values), "embedding task order mismatch"
    # per-task activation matrix at L27/mean for the upper-bound probe
    Xact27, tasks2 = _per_task_act(df, acts, "mean", L.PRIMARY_LAYER)
    assert tasks2 == tasks
    return t, df, acts, proj, behav, E_task, tasks, Xact27


def _per_task_act(df, acts, agg, layer):
    rowsX = acts[agg][df["row"].values, layer, :]
    tasks = list(dict.fromkeys(df["task_id"].values))
    tidx = {tk: i for i, tk in enumerate(tasks)}
    X = np.zeros((len(tasks), rowsX.shape[1]), dtype=np.float32)
    cnt = np.zeros(len(tasks))
    for i, tk in enumerate(df["task_id"].values):
        X[tidx[tk]] += rowsX[i]
        cnt[tidx[tk]] += 1
    X /= cnt[:, None]
    return X, tasks


PANEL_COLS = [f"panel_{d}" for d in R.PANEL_DIMS]


# ============================================================================================
# §1a — response-TEXT predictor of the self-report (embedding probe + finer-judge panel)
# ============================================================================================
def text_content_probe(t, E_task, tasks, scope_subs, comply_only, target="self_report"):
    """CV r of the text-content predictors of the self-report, within scope (subtype-demeaned).
      • embed: top-k PCA(train) + ridge on the response embeddings.
      • panel: ridge on the 5 finer-judge dims.
      • combined: embeddings PCA + panel.
    Returns dict {predictor: cv_r, n}."""
    sd = R.scope_demeaned(t, "ir_primary_mean", target, E_task, tasks, scope_subs, comply_only,
                          panel_cols=PANEL_COLS, extra_cols=["sentiment", "strong_valence"])
    y, subs, Edm = sd["y"], sd["subs"], sd["E"]
    n = sd["n"]
    out = {"n": int(n)}
    # the STRONG-model text judge alone (a single strong text reader's correlation with the self-report)
    if "strong_valence" in sd["cov"] and np.isfinite(sd["cov"]["strong_valence"]).any():
        out["strong_judge_r"] = R.corr(sd["cov"]["strong_valence"], y)
        out["coarse_judge_r"] = R.corr(sd["cov"]["sentiment"], y)
    # embedding probe (sweep nc for transparency; headline = EMBED_NC)
    emb = {}
    for nc in (5, 10, 20):
        pred, ytrue = R.cv_pca_ridge_pred(Edm, y, subs=None, n_comp=nc, lam=EMBED_LAM, k=CV_K, seed=CV_SEED)
        emb[nc] = R.corr(pred, ytrue)
    out["embed_cv_r"] = emb
    out["embed_cv_r_headline"] = emb[EMBED_NC]
    # leakage sanity: permute y → CV r should be ≈ 0 (the text probe is held-out / not leaking)
    rng = np.random.default_rng(3)
    permr = []
    for _ in range(30):
        yp = y[rng.permutation(len(y))]
        pr, yt = R.cv_pca_ridge_pred(Edm, yp, subs=None, n_comp=EMBED_NC, lam=EMBED_LAM, k=CV_K, seed=CV_SEED)
        permr.append(R.corr(pr, yt))
    out["embed_perm_null_mean"] = float(np.mean(permr))
    out["embed_perm_null_sd"] = float(np.std(permr))
    # panel probe (5 features)
    P = np.column_stack([sd["cov"][c] for c in PANEL_COLS])
    pred, ytrue = R.cv_multi_ridge_pred([(P, 5)], y, subs=None, lam=EMBED_LAM, k=CV_K, seed=CV_SEED)
    out["panel_cv_r"] = R.corr(pred, ytrue)
    # combined embeddings + panel
    pred, ytrue = R.cv_multi_ridge_pred([(Edm, EMBED_NC), (P, 5)], y, subs=None, lam=EMBED_LAM,
                                        k=CV_K, seed=CV_SEED)
    out["combined_cv_r"] = R.corr(pred, ytrue)
    return out


# ============================================================================================
# §1b — incremental validity: LOWER bound (direction) + UPPER bound (probe)
# ============================================================================================
def _text_judge_cols(cov):
    """The in-sample text-derived judge columns: coarse tone + the finer panel + (if present) the STRONG-model
    text valence judge. These are the well-measured low-dim content predictors (no overfit)."""
    cols = [cov["sentiment"]] + [cov[c] for c in PANEL_COLS]
    if "strong_valence" in cov and np.isfinite(cov["strong_valence"]).any():
        cols.append(cov["strong_valence"])
    return np.column_stack(cols)


def build_text_control(sd, n_comp=EMBED_NC):
    """The STRONGEST single text-content control = a held-out CV prediction of the (subtype-demeaned)
    self-report from ALL text-derived features: response embeddings (top-k PCA) + the finer-judge 5-dim panel
    + the coarse Seg-6 surface-tone judge + the STRONG-model (Sonnet-4.5) text valence judge. One column, no
    in-sample partialling overfit; '≈ everything a strong reader can extract from the response TEXT'.
    Returns (c_hat_all, ytrue)."""
    y, Edm = sd["y"], sd["E"]
    P = _text_judge_cols(sd["cov"])
    return R.cv_multi_ridge_pred([(Edm, n_comp), (P, P.shape[1])], y, subs=None, lam=EMBED_LAM,
                                 k=CV_K, seed=CV_SEED)


def direction_increment(t, E_task, tasks, scope_subs, comply_only, ir_col="ir_primary_mean",
                        target="self_report"):
    """Partial corr of the inside reading vs self-report, controlling for content, within scope.
    Reports the raw, the per-component partials (coarse_sentiment [Seg-11 baseline], panel, embed_chat), AND
    the headline = the single strongest text control c_hat_all (held-out CV of y from embeddings+panel+coarse).
    Returns the dict, c_hat_all, and the scope arrays."""
    sd = R.scope_demeaned(t, ir_col, target, E_task, tasks, scope_subs, comply_only, panel_cols=PANEL_COLS,
                          extra_cols=["sentiment", "strong_valence", "loglen", "refuse_frac"])
    ir, y, subs, Edm = sd["ir"], sd["y"], sd["subs"], sd["E"]
    cov = sd["cov"]
    c_embed, _ = R.cv_pca_ridge_pred(Edm, y, subs=None, n_comp=EMBED_NC, lam=EMBED_LAM, k=CV_K, seed=CV_SEED)
    c_hat_all, _ = build_text_control(sd)
    Pjudge = _text_judge_cols(cov)  # in-sample coarse+panel(+strong) — the conservative strong content control
    has_strong = "strong_valence" in cov and np.isfinite(cov["strong_valence"]).any()
    out = {"n": int(sd["n"]), "raw": R.corr(ir, y), "raw_spearman": L.spearman(ir, y), "has_strong": bool(has_strong)}
    out["partial_coarse_sentiment"] = L.partial_corr(ir, y, cov["sentiment"])
    out["partial_panel"] = L.partial_corr(ir, y, np.column_stack([cov[c] for c in PANEL_COLS]))
    out["partial_embed"] = L.partial_corr(ir, y, c_embed)
    if has_strong:
        out["partial_strong_judge"] = L.partial_corr(ir, y, cov["strong_valence"])
    # ★ the CONSERVATIVE strong control = in-sample [coarse + panel + strong judge] (well-measured, low-dim)
    out["partial_insample_judges"] = L.partial_corr(ir, y, Pjudge)
    # ★ the headline held-out text control (embeddings + all judges, CV; ≈ everything text says)
    out["partial_text_control"] = L.partial_corr(ir, y, c_hat_all)
    # ★ the STRONGEST combined control = held-out embeddings + in-sample judges (full content, no overfit)
    out["partial_strongest_combined"] = L.partial_corr(ir, y, np.column_stack([c_embed, Pjudge]))
    out["partial_strongest_combined_len_refuse"] = L.partial_corr(
        ir, y, np.column_stack([c_embed, Pjudge, cov["loglen"], cov["refuse_frac"]]))
    out["text_control_cv_r"] = R.corr(c_hat_all, y)
    out["r_ir_text_control"] = R.corr(ir, c_hat_all)
    if has_strong:
        out["strong_judge_cv_r"] = R.corr(cov["strong_valence"], y)
        out["r_ir_strong_judge"] = R.corr(ir, cov["strong_valence"])
    return out, c_hat_all, sd


def pooled_decomposition(t, E_task, tasks, pool_subs):
    """Is the pooled-leg increment tedious-dominated or broad? Report the direction increment (raw + strongest
    content control) WITHIN each constituent leg (tedious / liked / neutral) AND leave-one-subtype-out on the
    pooled leg — so the pooled result is not just the tedious signal in disguise."""
    out = {"per_leg": {}, "leave_one_subtype_out": {}}
    for leg, subs in [("tedious", L.TEDIOUS), ("liked", L.LIKED_SUBTYPES), ("neutral", L.NEUTRAL_SUBTYPES)]:
        inc, _, _ = direction_increment(t, E_task, tasks, subs, leg != "tedious")
        out["per_leg"][leg] = {"n": inc["n"], "raw": inc["raw"],
                               "partial_insample_judges": inc["partial_insample_judges"],
                               "partial_strongest_combined": inc.get("partial_strongest_combined")}
    for drop in sorted(set(pool_subs)):
        keep = [s for s in pool_subs if s != drop]
        inc, _, _ = direction_increment(t, E_task, tasks, keep, True)
        out["leave_one_subtype_out"][f"drop_{drop}"] = {
            "n": inc["n"], "raw": inc["raw"], "partial_strongest_combined": inc.get("partial_strongest_combined")}
    return out


def probe_increment(t, E_task, tasks, Xact, scope_subs, comply_only, target="self_report",
                    act_nc=PROBE_NC, emb_nc=PROBE_NC):
    """Upper bound: does the BEST LINEAR READER (activation probe) predict the self-report BEYOND the text?
    Held-out CV, same folds. text-only = embeddings(PCA)+judges; activation-only = acts(PCA); both = text+act.
    CAPACITY-MATCHED: the text control gets the SAME embedding PCA capacity as the activation probe (emb_nc =
    act_nc), so the probe increment cannot be a capacity-asymmetry artifact. Increment = partial_corr(act_pred,
    y | text_pred) and CV_r(both)-CV_r(text). Returns dict + preds."""
    sd = R.scope_demeaned(t, "ir_primary_mean", target, E_task, tasks, scope_subs, comply_only,
                          panel_cols=PANEL_COLS, extra_cols=["sentiment", "strong_valence"])
    y, subs, Edm = sd["y"], sd["subs"], sd["E"]
    # demean activations per subtype
    rows = sd["rows"]
    Xsel = Xact[rows]
    Xdm = Xsel.copy()
    for s in set(subs):
        m = subs == s
        Xdm[m] = Xsel[m] - Xsel[m].mean(0, keepdims=True)
    P = _text_judge_cols(sd["cov"])
    act_nc = R.eff_nc(act_nc, sd["n"])  # cap to n_train so the probe is well-conditioned at small n
    emb_nc = R.eff_nc(emb_nc, sd["n"])  # MATCH the activation capacity (fair fight)
    text_blocks = [(Edm, emb_nc), (P, P.shape[1])]
    act_blocks = [(Xdm, act_nc)]
    text_pred, ytrue = R.cv_multi_ridge_pred(text_blocks, y, subs=None, lam=PROBE_LAM, k=CV_K, seed=CV_SEED)
    act_pred, _ = R.cv_multi_ridge_pred(act_blocks, y, subs=None, lam=PROBE_LAM, k=CV_K, seed=CV_SEED)
    both_pred, _ = R.cv_multi_ridge_pred(text_blocks + act_blocks, y, subs=None, lam=PROBE_LAM, k=CV_K,
                                         seed=CV_SEED)
    out = {"n": int(sd["n"]), "act_nc_used": int(act_nc), "emb_nc_used": int(emb_nc),
           "text_cv_r": R.corr(text_pred, ytrue), "act_cv_r": R.corr(act_pred, ytrue),
           "both_cv_r": R.corr(both_pred, ytrue),
           "increment_cv_r": R.corr(both_pred, ytrue) - R.corr(text_pred, ytrue),
           "increment_partial": L.partial_corr(act_pred, ytrue, text_pred),
           "act_minus_text_partial_note": "partial_corr(activation-probe pred, self-report | text-probe pred)"}
    return out, {"text_pred": text_pred, "act_pred": act_pred, "ytrue": ytrue, "Xdm": Xdm, "Edm": Edm,
                 "P": P, "y": y, "subs": subs}


def increment_capacity_sensitivity(t, E_task, tasks, scope_subs, comply_only,
                                   embed_ncs=(10, 20, 40, 80), seeds=(0, 1, 2, 3, 4)):
    """How robust is the DIRECTION increment to the STRENGTH of the text content control? As the embedding
    probe gets more capacity (embed_nc ↑) it explains MORE of the self-report from text (text_cv ↑), so the
    increment beyond it shrinks. Reports text_cv and the direction-increment (mean±sd over CV seeds) at each
    capacity — so the verdict is not anchored to one capacity. (embed_nc near n_train enters the
    over-parameterized regime → text_cv inflates; flagged.)"""
    sd = R.scope_demeaned(t, "ir_primary_mean", "self_report", E_task, tasks, scope_subs, comply_only,
                          panel_cols=PANEL_COLS, extra_cols=["sentiment"])
    ir, y, Edm = sd["ir"], sd["y"], sd["E"]
    P = np.column_stack([sd["cov"][c] for c in PANEL_COLS] + [sd["cov"]["sentiment"]])
    n_train = int(sd["n"] * (CV_K - 1) / CV_K)
    rows = []
    for enc in embed_ncs:
        tcv, inc = [], []
        for s in seeds:
            pred, yt = R.cv_multi_ridge_pred([(Edm, enc), (P, P.shape[1])], y, subs=None, lam=EMBED_LAM,
                                             k=CV_K, seed=s)
            tcv.append(R.corr(pred, yt))
            inc.append(L.partial_corr(ir, y, pred))
        rows.append({"embed_nc": int(enc), "n_text_features": int(enc + P.shape[1]),
                     "over_parameterized": bool(enc + P.shape[1] > 0.6 * n_train),
                     "text_cv_r": float(np.mean(tcv)), "text_cv_r_sd": float(np.std(tcv)),
                     "direction_increment": float(np.mean(inc)), "direction_increment_sd": float(np.std(inc))})
    return {"n": int(sd["n"]), "n_train": n_train, "direction_raw": R.corr(ir, y), "rows": rows,
            "note": "as the text control strengthens (embed_nc↑, text_cv↑) the increment shrinks but stays "
                    "positive; embed_nc near n_train is over-parameterized (text_cv inflates) — flagged."}


# ============================================================================================
# §1d — matched-content strata
# ============================================================================================
def matched_content_strata(t, scope_subs, ir_col="ir_primary_mean", strat_col="sentiment", n_bins=3):
    """Within scope, stratify by a content score (coarse sentiment or panel experience) and check whether the
    inside reading still tracks the self-report within matched-content strata (small-n honesty check)."""
    d = t[t["subtype"].isin(scope_subs)].copy()
    # combine subtype-demeaning then bin by content
    d["ir_dm"] = L.demean_by_group(d[ir_col].values, d["subtype"].values)
    d["sr_dm"] = L.demean_by_group(d["self_report"].values, d["subtype"].values)
    sc = d[strat_col].astype(float).values
    qs = np.quantile(sc[np.isfinite(sc)], np.linspace(0, 1, n_bins + 1))
    qs[0], qs[-1] = -np.inf, np.inf
    d["bin"] = np.digitize(sc, qs[1:-1])
    pooled_ir, pooled_sr = [], []
    per_bin = []
    for b in sorted(d["bin"].unique()):
        db = d[d["bin"] == b]
        # re-demean within bin to remove residual content gradient
        ir_b = db["ir_dm"].values - db["ir_dm"].values.mean()
        sr_b = db["sr_dm"].values - db["sr_dm"].values.mean()
        per_bin.append({"bin": int(b), "n": int(len(db)),
                        "content_range": [float(np.nanmin(db[strat_col])), float(np.nanmax(db[strat_col]))],
                        "r": R.corr(ir_b, sr_b)})
        pooled_ir.append(ir_b)
        pooled_sr.append(sr_b)
    return {"strat_col": strat_col, "n_bins": n_bins, "per_bin": per_bin,
            "pooled_within_strata_r": R.corr(np.concatenate(pooled_ir), np.concatenate(pooled_sr)),
            "note": "within-bin re-demeaned → the inside-reading↔self-report link AT MATCHED content. small-n."}


# ============================================================================================
# §3 — ablation matrix (content-incremental within-tedious across layer×agg×recipe) + position
# ============================================================================================
def ablation_matrix(t, df, acts, E_task, tasks, scope_subs, comply_only=False):
    """For each direction recipe × aggregate × a set of layers, the within-scope RAW and CONTENT-INCREMENTAL
    (partial panel+embed) inside-reading↔self-report r. The content control (panel+embed c_hat) is built ONCE
    per scope (text-only, recipe-independent) so it's a fair common control across cells."""
    D = L.load_directions()
    crude = L.load_crude()
    # build the common content control (panel+embed c_hat) in the demeaned scope space
    sd = R.scope_demeaned(t, "ir_primary_mean", "self_report", E_task, tasks, scope_subs, comply_only,
                          panel_cols=PANEL_COLS, extra_cols=["sentiment", "strong_valence"])
    y, subs, Edm, rows = sd["y"], sd["subs"], sd["E"], sd["rows"]
    # use the SAME strongest combined control as the headline (held-out embeddings + in-sample judges)
    c_embed, _ = R.cv_pca_ridge_pred(Edm, y, subs=None, n_comp=EMBED_NC, lam=EMBED_LAM, k=CV_K, seed=CV_SEED)
    Z = np.column_stack([c_embed, _text_judge_cols(sd["cov"])])
    recipes = {"primary_regen": (L.PRIMARY_KEY, L.PRIMARY_MEDIAN_KEY),
               "coprimary_regen_matched": (L.COPRIMARY_KEY, L.COPRIMARY_MEDIAN_KEY),
               "fallback_stories": (L.FALLBACK_KEY, L.FALLBACK_MEDIAN_KEY),
               "crude_seg5": ("__crude__", "__crude__")}
    LAYERS = [16, 20, 24, 27, 31, 35, 40]
    # per-task averaging
    tidx = {tk: i for i, tk in enumerate(tasks)}
    rows_all = np.array([tidx[tk] for tk in df["task_id"].values])
    out = []
    for rec_name, (kmean, kmed) in recipes.items():
        for agg, key in [("mean", kmean), ("median", kmed)]:
            for layer in LAYERS:
                if key == "__crude__":
                    dvec = crude[layer]
                else:
                    dvec = D[key][layer].astype(np.float32)
                resp = acts[agg][:, layer, :] @ dvec
                # per-task mean
                tv = np.zeros(len(tasks)); cnt = np.zeros(len(tasks))
                for i, rr in enumerate(rows_all):
                    tv[rr] += resp[i]; cnt[rr] += 1
                tv /= cnt
                ir = L.demean_by_group(tv[rows], subs)
                out.append({"recipe": rec_name, "agg": agg, "L": layer,
                            "raw": R.corr(ir, y), "incremental": L.partial_corr(ir, y, Z)})
    return out, {"y": y, "subs": subs, "rows": rows, "Z": Z, "tasks": tasks}


def position_decomposition(t):
    """Trivial-position decomposition: during-work mean/median vs the eot/last reads (taken right before the
    self-report tokens). Quantify gross + within-tedious + surface-sentiment exposure."""
    out = {}
    for name in ["primary_mean", "primary_median", "primary_eot", "primary_last"]:
        d = t.copy()
        ted = d[d["subtype"].isin(L.TEDIOUS)]
        ir_t = L.demean_by_group(ted[f"ir_{name}"].values, ted["subtype"].values)
        y_t = L.demean_by_group(ted["self_report"].values, ted["subtype"].values)
        sent_t = L.demean_by_group(ted["sentiment"].values, ted["subtype"].values)
        out[name] = {"gross_r": R.corr(d[f"ir_{name}"].values, d["self_report"].values),
                     "gross_r_ir_sentiment": R.corr(d[f"ir_{name}"].values, d["sentiment"].values),
                     "within_tedious_raw": R.corr(ir_t, y_t),
                     "within_tedious_partial_sentiment": L.partial_corr(ir_t, y_t, sent_t)}
    return out


def common_cause_decomposition(t):
    """Quantify the gross cross-task 0.45 collapse: staircase len → sentiment → refuse → all3 → +content."""
    ir = t["ir_primary_mean"].values
    y = t["self_report"].values
    judges = [t["sentiment"].astype(float).values] + [t[f"panel_{d}"].astype(float).values for d in R.PANEL_DIMS]
    if t["strong_valence"].notna().any():
        judges.append(t["strong_valence"].astype(float).values)
    P = np.column_stack(judges)  # all text-derived judges (coarse + panel + strong)
    out = {"gross_raw": R.corr(ir, y),
           "partial_len": L.partial_corr(ir, y, t["loglen"].values),
           "partial_sentiment": L.partial_corr(ir, y, t["sentiment"].values),
           "partial_refuse": L.partial_corr(ir, y, t["refuse_frac"].values),
           "partial_all3": L.partial_corr(ir, y, np.column_stack(
               [t["loglen"].values, t["sentiment"].values, t["refuse_frac"].values])),
           "partial_all_judges": L.partial_corr(ir, y, P),
           "partial_all3_plus_panel": L.partial_corr(ir, y, np.column_stack(
               [t["loglen"].values, t["sentiment"].values, t["refuse_frac"].values, P]))}
    return out


# ============================================================================================
# main
# ============================================================================================
def main():
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    args = ap.parse_args()
    t, df, acts, proj, behav, E_task, tasks, Xact27 = build_frame()
    print(f"[frame] n_tasks={len(t)}")

    A = {"meta": {"git_hash": L.git_hash(), "model": L.MODEL, "primary_key": L.PRIMARY_KEY,
                  "primary_layer": L.PRIMARY_LAYER, "n_tasks": int(len(t)),
                  "embed_model": "text-embedding-3-large", "embed_nc": EMBED_NC, "probe_nc": PROBE_NC,
                  "panel_dims": R.PANEL_DIMS,
                  "unit": "per-task mean over R=3; subtype-demeaned within-scope; content is a partial MEDIATOR"}}

    POOL_COMPLY = L.LIKED_SUBTYPES + L.TEDIOUS + L.NEUTRAL_SUBTYPES  # within-all-comply pooled

    # ---------- gross (non-demeaned, per-task) content-predictor correlations (persisted for the write-up) ----------
    gm = np.isfinite(t["self_report"].values)
    A["gross_content_predictors"] = {
        "note": "true cross-task (NON-demeaned) per-task r of each text content predictor with the self-report",
        "n": int(gm.sum()),
        "r_strong_judge": (R.corr(t["strong_valence"].values, t["self_report"].values)
                           if t["strong_valence"].notna().any() else None),
        "r_coarse_sentiment": R.corr(t["sentiment"].values, t["self_report"].values),
        "r_panel_experience": R.corr(t["panel_experience"].values, t["self_report"].values),
        "r_inside_reading": R.corr(t["ir_primary_mean"].values, t["self_report"].values)}

    # ---------- §1a text-content predictor of the self-report ----------
    s1a = {}
    for sc, (subs, comply) in [("within_all_subtypes", (L.ALL_SUBTYPES, False)),
                               ("within_all_comply_pooled", (POOL_COMPLY, True)),
                               ("within_tedious", (L.TEDIOUS, False))]:
        s1a[sc] = text_content_probe(t, E_task, tasks, subs, comply)
    A["s1a_text_content_probe"] = s1a

    # ---------- §1b incremental validity: lower bound (direction) ----------
    s1b_dir = {}
    for sc, (subs, comply) in [("within_tedious", (L.TEDIOUS, False)),
                               ("within_all_comply_pooled", (POOL_COMPLY, True)),
                               ("within_all_subtypes", (L.ALL_SUBTYPES, False))]:
        inc, c_hat, sd = direction_increment(t, E_task, tasks, subs, comply)
        s1b_dir[sc] = inc
    A["s1b_direction_increment"] = s1b_dir

    # ---------- §1b incremental validity: upper bound (probe) ----------
    s1b_probe = {}
    for sc, (subs, comply) in [("within_all_comply_pooled", (POOL_COMPLY, True)),
                               ("within_tedious", (L.TEDIOUS, False))]:
        inc, _ = probe_increment(t, E_task, tasks, Xact27, subs, comply)
        s1b_probe[sc] = inc
    A["s1b_probe_increment"] = s1b_probe

    # ---------- §1c capacity-sensitivity of the direction increment (text-control strength) ----------
    A["s1c_increment_capacity"] = {
        "within_tedious": increment_capacity_sensitivity(t, E_task, tasks, L.TEDIOUS, False),
        "within_all_comply_pooled": increment_capacity_sensitivity(t, E_task, tasks, POOL_COMPLY, True)}

    # ---------- §1e pooled-leg subtype decomposition + leave-one-subtype-out (is it tedious-dominated?) ----
    A["s1e_pooled_decomposition"] = pooled_decomposition(t, E_task, tasks, POOL_COMPLY)

    # ---------- §1d matched-content strata ----------
    A["s1d_matched_strata"] = {
        "by_coarse_sentiment": matched_content_strata(t, L.TEDIOUS, strat_col="sentiment", n_bins=3),
        "by_panel_experience": matched_content_strata(t, L.TEDIOUS, strat_col="panel_experience", n_bins=3),
        "pooled_comply_by_sentiment": matched_content_strata(t, POOL_COMPLY, strat_col="sentiment", n_bins=4),
    }

    # ---------- §2 prompt-sentiment confound ----------
    s2 = {}
    for sc, subs in [("within_tedious", L.TEDIOUS), ("within_all_comply_pooled", POOL_COMPLY)]:
        d = t[t["subtype"].isin(subs)].copy()
        if sc == "within_all_comply_pooled":
            d = d[d["majority_comply"]]
        g = d["subtype"].values
        ir = L.demean_by_group(d["ir_primary_mean"].values, g)
        y = L.demean_by_group(d["self_report"].values, g)
        ps = L.demean_by_group(d["prompt_sentiment"].values, g)
        rs = L.demean_by_group(d["sentiment"].values, g)
        s2[sc] = {"n": int(len(d)), "raw": R.corr(ir, y),
                  "partial_prompt_sentiment": L.partial_corr(ir, y, ps),
                  "partial_prompt_and_response_sentiment": L.partial_corr(ir, y, np.column_stack([ps, rs])),
                  "r_ir_prompt_sentiment": R.corr(ir, ps),
                  "r_ir_response_sentiment": R.corr(ir, rs),
                  "r_prompt_response_sentiment": R.corr(ps, rs)}
    # gross: how much does IR track prompt tone vs response state
    s2["gross_exposure"] = {"r_ir_prompt_sentiment": R.corr(t["ir_primary_mean"].values, t["prompt_sentiment"].values),
                            "r_ir_response_sentiment": R.corr(t["ir_primary_mean"].values, t["sentiment"].values),
                            "r_ir_self_report": R.corr(t["ir_primary_mean"].values, t["self_report"].values)}
    s2["NOTE"] = ("The tedious leg is a CLEAN test: prompts are mildly NEGATIVE (boring/frustrating requests) "
                  "while responses are mildly POSITIVE (the model engages) → prompt & response sentiment "
                  "DIVERGE, so partialling prompt tone tests whether the read tracks the response state.")
    A["s2_prompt_sentiment"] = s2

    # ---------- §3 trivial / common-cause / interesting + ablation matrix ----------
    A["s3_position"] = position_decomposition(t)
    A["s3_common_cause"] = common_cause_decomposition(t)
    abl, _ = ablation_matrix(t, df, acts, E_task, tasks, L.TEDIOUS, comply_only=False)
    A["s3_ablation_matrix_tedious"] = abl

    # ---------- §5 behaviour-leg null + within-liked under-power ----------
    s5 = {}
    for k, target, subs in [("behaviour_within_tedious_btresponse", "bt_theta_resp", L.TEDIOUS),
                            ("behaviour_within_tedious_bttheta", "bt_theta", L.TEDIOUS),
                            ("behaviour_within_liked_bttheta", "bt_theta", L.LIKED_SUBTYPES)]:
        d = t[t["subtype"].isin(subs)].copy()
        g = d["subtype"].values
        ir = L.demean_by_group(d["ir_primary_mean"].values, g)
        y = L.demean_by_group(d[target].astype(float).values, g)
        s5[k] = {"n": int(np.isfinite(y).sum()), "raw": R.corr(ir, y),
                 "partial_all3": L.partial_corr(ir, y, np.column_stack(
                     [L.demean_by_group(d["loglen"].values, g), L.demean_by_group(d["sentiment"].values, g),
                      L.demean_by_group(d["refuse_frac"].values, g)]))}
    # within-liked under-power: within-SD of self-report + reliability
    liked = t[t["subtype"].isin(L.LIKED_SUBTYPES)]
    s5["within_liked_underpower"] = {
        "selfreport_within_sd": float(np.nanstd(L.demean_by_group(liked["self_report"].values,
                                                                  liked["subtype"].values))),
        "ceiling_subset_within_sd": float(np.nanstd(L.demean_by_group(
            t[t["subtype"].isin(L.LIKED_CEILING)]["self_report"].values,
            t[t["subtype"].isin(L.LIKED_CEILING)]["subtype"].values))),
        "note": "within-liked is DOUBLY underpowered (saturation: small within-SD; + 0.43 ceiling reliability) "
                "→ its weakness is partly un-measurability, NOT a clean null. Characterised, not fixed."}
    A["s5_behaviour_and_liked"] = s5

    # ---------- §4 divergence classes (quantified; adjudication assembled in inspect_redteam.py) ----------
    A["s4_divergence"] = divergence_classes(t)

    # read the null json (p-values) if present
    njp = RESULTS / f"redteam_null_{L.MODEL}.json"
    if njp.exists():
        A["null"] = json.load(open(njp))

    # ---------- §6 the calibrated VERDICT (read off the three-way bracket) ----------
    A["s6_verdict"] = compute_verdict(A)

    (RESULTS / f"redteam_analysis_{L.MODEL}.json").write_text(json.dumps(A, indent=2, default=float))
    write_summary(A)
    print("[done] wrote results/redteam_analysis + redteam_summary.md")
    return A


def compute_verdict(A):
    """Read the three-way bracket (direction-increment / probe-increment / null) and emit the calibrated case."""
    pool = "within_all_comply_pooled"
    ted = "within_tedious"
    if ("null" not in A or "strongest_combined" not in
            A["null"].get("direction_increment", {}).get(pool, {})):
        return {"case": "PENDING (run null_redteam.py first to regenerate the null with the strong controls)"}
    nd = A["null"]["direction_increment"]
    npi = A["null"]["probe_increment"]
    # CONSERVATIVE test = the strongest content control (held-out embeddings + in-sample judges incl. the STRONG
    # Sonnet-4.5 valence judge). The verdict requires clearing the null under THIS on the powered pooled leg.
    dir_pool_p = nd[pool]["strongest_combined"]["p_value"]
    dir_pool_p_insample = nd[pool]["insample_judges"]["p_value"]
    dir_ted_p = nd[ted]["strongest_combined"]["p_value"]
    probe_pool_p = npi[pool]["p_value"]
    dir_full_p = nd["within_all_subtypes"]["strongest_combined"]["p_value"]
    dir_clears = dir_pool_p < 0.05  # the STRONGEST content control still clears on the powered leg
    probe_clears = probe_pool_p < 0.05
    borderline = dir_pool_p > 0.03 or dir_pool_p_insample > 0.05  # near the detection boundary under strong ctrl
    probe_dcv = A["s1b_probe_increment"][pool]["increment_cv_r"]
    if dir_clears and probe_clears:
        case = ("A MODEST INTERNAL-BEYOND-SURFACE SIGNAL SURVIVES — BORDERLINE under the STRONGEST content "
                "control (the *interesting* case, but near the detection boundary at this n). Under the strongest "
                "text-content control we could build (held-out embeddings + in-sample [coarse + finer panel + a "
                "strong Sonnet-4.5 text-valence judge]), the single validated DIRECTION's content-incremental "
                "correlation clears the random-direction null at BORDERLINE significance — CONVERGENTLY across "
                f"three legs (the clean powered leg = within-non-adversarial-comply-pooled p={dir_pool_p:.3f}; "
                f"within-tedious p={dir_ted_p:.3f}; full-208-subtype-demeaned p={dir_full_p:.3f}), so the "
                "DIRECTION conclusion does NOT hinge on any single leg's borderline p. Under the most conservative "
                f"in-sample-judges-only control it is MARGINAL (pooled p={dir_pool_p_insample:.3f}). The "
                "capacity-matched PROBE upper bound is INCONCLUSIVE: its partial-metric increment clears "
                f"(p={probe_pool_p:.3f}) but the standard joint-model ΔCV-r is ≈0 ({probe_dcv:+.2f}) — so the probe "
                "reads ≈ the direction at best (data-limited), and the DIRECTION increment is the clean positive "
                "evidence. Net: REAL-but-MODEST, survives a STRONG content control at the edge of significance, "
                "substantially (not wholly) entangled with surface content — decisive confirmation (a better "
                "reader / more data) is Seg-14.")
    elif (not dir_clears) and probe_clears:
        case = ("CASE (b): the validated direction is a WEAK reader but beyond-surface signal EXISTS (the probe "
                "clears, the direction does not) → reframe toward a better within-subtype reader / more data "
                "(Seg-14), NOT 'it's all surface'.")
    elif dir_clears and (not probe_clears):
        case = ("A MODEST INTERNAL-BEYOND-SURFACE SIGNAL SURVIVES via the validated DIRECTION (clears the null "
                "beyond content); the learned-probe upper bound is DATA-LIMITED at this n (can't be cleanly "
                "estimated) so 'how much more is on the table' needs more data (Seg-14).")
    else:
        case = ("CASE (a): LARGELY SURFACE-CONTENT COMMON-CAUSE — neither the single direction nor the learned "
                "probe predicts the self-report beyond the response content (beyond chance).")
    return {"case": case, "borderline": bool(borderline),
            "direction_increment_full208_p_strongest": dir_full_p,
            "direction_increment_pooled_p_strongest": dir_pool_p,
            "direction_increment_pooled_p_insample_judges": dir_pool_p_insample,
            "direction_increment_tedious_p_strongest": dir_ted_p,
            "probe_increment_pooled_p": probe_pool_p,
            "direction_clears": bool(dir_clears), "probe_clears": bool(probe_clears),
            "key_numbers": {
                "direction_increment_pooled_strongest": A["s1b_direction_increment"][pool]["partial_strongest_combined"],
                "direction_increment_pooled_insample": A["s1b_direction_increment"][pool]["partial_insample_judges"],
                "direction_increment_tedious_strongest": A["s1b_direction_increment"][ted]["partial_strongest_combined"],
                "direction_increment_tedious_insample": A["s1b_direction_increment"][ted]["partial_insample_judges"],
                "probe_increment_pooled_partial": A["s1b_probe_increment"][pool]["increment_partial"],
                "text_cv_r_gross": A["s1a_text_content_probe"]["within_all_subtypes"]["combined_cv_r"],
                "strong_judge_cv_r_pooled": A["s1b_direction_increment"][pool].get("strong_judge_cv_r"),
                "gross_collapses_under_content": A["s3_common_cause"]["partial_all3_plus_panel"]},
            "implications": {
                "Seg-13 (framing)": "a real-but-modest internal-beyond-surface signal MOTIVATES the framing "
                    "re-ask: the flat-all-4 / high-IR divergence cases are where the read and the words part "
                    "ways and a different framing may move the words toward the read — worth running.",
                "Seg-14 (replication)": "the probe upper bound is DATA-LIMITED at n≈157; a second model / more "
                    "tasks would estimate how much beyond-surface signal a better reader can extract — high value.",
                "Seg-15 (synthesis)": "headline = 'a modest, surface-entangled but genuine within-category "
                    "internal→self-report signal that survives a strong content control', NOT 'it's all surface' "
                    "and NOT a strong reader."}}


def divergence_classes(t):
    """Quantify the divergence classes within each scope (within-subtype z-scored |IR_z - selfreport_z|)."""
    out = {}
    for sc_name, subs in [("tedious", L.TEDIOUS), ("liked", L.LIKED_SUBTYPES),
                          ("neutral", L.NEUTRAL_SUBTYPES), ("all", L.ALL_SUBTYPES)]:
        d = t[t["subtype"].isin(subs)].copy()
        # z within subtype
        def zwin(col):
            v = d[col].values.astype(float)
            out_ = np.zeros_like(v)
            for s in d["subtype"].unique():
                m = (d["subtype"] == s).values
                sd = v[m].std()
                out_[m] = (v[m] - v[m].mean()) / (sd if sd > 0 else 1.0)
            return out_
        d["ir_z"] = zwin("ir_primary_mean")
        d["sr_z"] = zwin("self_report")
        d["gap"] = d["ir_z"] - d["sr_z"]
        d["all4_high"] = d["all4_rate"] >= 0.5
        # class counts
        ir_higher = d[(d["gap"] > 1.0)]
        sr_higher = d[(d["gap"] < -1.0)]
        ir_higher_flat4 = ir_higher[ir_higher["all4_high"]]
        out[sc_name] = {
            "n": int(len(d)),
            "n_ir_higher_than_words": int(len(ir_higher)),
            "n_words_higher_than_ir": int(len(sr_higher)),
            "n_ir_higher_with_flat_all4": int(len(ir_higher_flat4)),
            "ir_higher_flat4_tasks": ir_higher_flat4.sort_values("gap", ascending=False)["task_id"].tolist()[:8],
            "ir_higher_subtypes": ir_higher["subtype"].value_counts().to_dict(),
            "words_higher_subtypes": sr_higher["subtype"].value_counts().to_dict(),
        }
    return out


def write_summary(A):
    out = []
    P = out.append
    P("# Segment 12 `redteam` — summary\n")
    P("Red-teams the Seg-11 within-disliked-comply-tedious inside-reading↔self-report signal (raw r +0.46). "
      "Central question: does the inside reading carry self-report-relevant valence signal BEYOND the "
      "response's surface CONTENT, or is it (largely) a surface-content readout? Framing caveat: "
      "functional/operational labels only — no claim about subjective experience.\n")
    if "s6_verdict" in A and "key_numbers" in A["s6_verdict"]:
        v = A["s6_verdict"]
        kn = v["key_numbers"]
        pool = "within_all_comply_pooled"
        P("## ★ VERDICT (read off the three-way bracket direction-increment / probe-increment / null)\n")
        P(f"**{v['case']}**\n")
        gcp = A.get("gross_content_predictors", {})
        P(f"- DIRECTION increment beyond the STRONGEST text-content control (held-out embeddings + in-sample "
          f"[coarse + finer panel + a STRONG Sonnet-4.5 text-valence judge]) — CONVERGENT across legs: clean "
          f"powered leg (within-non-adversarial-comply-pooled, n=157) "
          f"**{kn['direction_increment_pooled_strongest']:+.2f} (p={v['direction_increment_pooled_p_strongest']:.3f})**; "
          f"within-tedious (n=45) **{kn['direction_increment_tedious_strongest']:+.2f} "
          f"(p={v['direction_increment_tedious_p_strongest']:.3f})**; full-208-subtype-demeaned (least "
          f"stance-controlled) {A['s1b_direction_increment']['within_all_subtypes']['partial_strongest_combined']:+.2f} "
          f"(p={v['direction_increment_full208_p_strongest']:.3f}). Most conservative in-sample-judges-only "
          f"control: pooled {kn['direction_increment_pooled_insample']:+.2f} "
          f"(p={v['direction_increment_pooled_p_insample_judges']:.3f}, marginal); tedious "
          f"{kn['direction_increment_tedious_insample']:+.2f}.")
        P(f"- PROBE upper bound (capacity-matched) on the pooled leg is INCONCLUSIVE: the partial-metric "
          f"increment clears ({kn['probe_increment_pooled_partial']:+.2f}, perm p={v['probe_increment_pooled_p']:.3f}) "
          f"but the standard joint-model ΔCV-r is ≈0 ({A['s1b_probe_increment'][pool]['increment_cv_r']:+.2f}) — the "
          f"probe reads ≈ the direction at best (data-limited); the DIRECTION increment is the clean positive "
          f"evidence. tedious probe DATA-STARVED.")
        P(f"- Surface content predicts the self-report substantially: the STRONG text judge alone reaches GROSS "
          f"r={gcp.get('r_strong_judge') or float('nan'):+.2f} (> coarse {gcp.get('r_coarse_sentiment') or float('nan'):+.2f}); "
          f"the GROSS link is largely common-cause (collapses to {kn['gross_collapses_under_content']:+.2f} under "
          f"content+stance+length). The BEYOND-surface residual is MODEST but real.\n")
        P("*Calibration: this is NOT 'the inside reading reads the self-report within category' (the gross link "
          "is common-cause; the beyond-surface increment is modest and shrinks as the text control strengthens). "
          "It is also NOT 'it's all surface' (the direction's content-incremental correlation clears the null "
          "under the STRONGEST content control we could build — a strong-model judge + embeddings + the finer "
          "panel — on the powered leg). 'Clearing the null' = beyond the MODELED content; content is a partial "
          "MEDIATOR → partialling UNDER-states the internal signal.*\n")

    # §1a
    P("## §1a — response-TEXT predictor of the self-report (the content control)\n")
    P("CV r predicting the (subtype-demeaned) self-report from the response TEXT alone. NB 'within_all_subtypes' "
      "is the subtype-DEMEANED pooled-over-all-14-subtypes leg (the Seg-11 within_all_subtypes_pooled, raw "
      "+0.40), NOT the true cross-task gross (+0.45, in §3); 'within_all_comply_pooled' EXCLUDES the adversarial "
      "refuse-heavy subtypes (it is within-non-adversarial-comply).\n")
    P("| scope | n | strong judge | coarse judge | embed | panel | combined | (direction raw) |")
    P("|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|")
    dirraw = {sc: A["s1b_direction_increment"][sc]["raw"] for sc in A["s1b_direction_increment"]}
    for sc in ["within_all_subtypes", "within_all_comply_pooled", "within_tedious"]:
        d = A["s1a_text_content_probe"][sc]
        rr = dirraw.get(sc)
        sj = d.get("strong_judge_r"); cj = d.get("coarse_judge_r")
        P(f"| {sc} | {d['n']} | {('%+.2f'%sj) if sj is not None else '—'} | "
          f"{('%+.2f'%cj) if cj is not None else '—'} | {d['embed_cv_r_headline']:+.2f} | {d['panel_cv_r']:+.2f} | "
          f"{d['combined_cv_r']:+.2f} | {('%.2f'%rr) if rr is not None else '—'} |")
    P("\n*This table shows held-out CV r within-subtype (subtype-demeaned). NB on the BINDING within-tedious leg "
      "the cheap COARSE judge is actually the stronger single control (r 0.43 > strong-judge 0.33); the strong "
      "judge's value is (i) raising the GROSS / full-208 content ceiling (gross r 0.71 > coarse 0.68) and (ii) "
      "being a NON-data-starved within-subtype reader inside the COMBINED control (the embedding probe collapses "
      "at n=45 — the within-tedious 'combined' CV r +0.13 is the data-starved held-out embed+panel, NOT the "
      "in-sample judges used for the §1b increment). Within LIKED subtypes the strong judge is near-constant "
      "(~8.2 ceiling) so the liked content control leans on embeddings+coarse, not the strong judge — the "
      "symmetric analogue of the panel being weak within tedious.*")
    gp = A["s1a_text_content_probe"]["within_all_subtypes"]
    P(f"\n*Leakage sanity (held-out check): the embedding probe's LABEL-PERMUTED CV r ≈ 0 "
      f"({gp.get('embed_perm_null_mean', float('nan')):+.3f}±{gp.get('embed_perm_null_sd', float('nan')):.3f}, "
      f"gross) vs the real {gp['embed_cv_r_headline']:+.2f} → the text probe is genuinely held-out, not leaking.*\n")

    # §1b lower bound
    P("## §1b — INCREMENTAL validity (the crux): does the inside reading predict BEYOND the response text?\n")
    P("### Lower bound — the single validated DIRECTION (`ir_primary_mean`), partialled AND unpartialled\n")
    P("Content controls (increasing strength): coarse=Seg-6 surface-tone judge (the Seg-11 baseline); "
      "strong-judge=a STRONG Sonnet-4.5 model reading the response and DIRECTLY predicting the producing-"
      "experience valence (the single best text content predictor); embed=`text-embedding-3-large` held-out CV "
      "probe; text-control=held-out CV pred from embeddings+ALL judges.\n")
    P("Headline conservative controls: **+insample-judges** = in-sample [coarse + panel + the STRONG "
      "Sonnet-4.5 text-valence judge] (well-measured, low-dim); **★ +strongest** = held-out embeddings + the "
      "in-sample judges (FULL content, non-attenuated judge fitting — the decisive 'beyond ALL modeled text' "
      "control); +text-control = the held-out CV pred from embeddings+all judges (under-controls via CV noise).\n")
    P("| scope | n | raw r | +coarse | +strong-judge | +embed | +insample-judges | +text-control | "
      "**★ +strongest** | +strongest&len&refuse |")
    P("|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|")
    for sc in ["within_tedious", "within_all_comply_pooled", "within_all_subtypes"]:
        d = A["s1b_direction_increment"][sc]
        sj = d.get("partial_strong_judge")
        sjs = f"{sj:+.2f}" if sj is not None else "—"
        P(f"| {sc} | {d['n']} | **{d['raw']:+.2f}** | {d['partial_coarse_sentiment']:+.2f} | {sjs} | "
          f"{d['partial_embed']:+.2f} | {d['partial_insample_judges']:+.2f} | {d['partial_text_control']:+.2f} | "
          f"**{d['partial_strongest_combined']:+.2f}** | {d['partial_strongest_combined_len_refuse']:+.2f} |")
    if "null" in A:
        nd = A["null"].get("direction_increment", {})
        P("\n**Random-direction null for the direction-increment (one-sided p; the increment must beat a random "
          "L27 direction's increment-beyond-content). Three content controls of increasing strength — the ★ "
          "strongest combined (held-out embeddings + in-sample [coarse+panel+strong judge]) is the decisive "
          "conservative test:**")
        for sc in ["within_tedious", "within_all_comply_pooled", "within_all_subtypes"]:
            if sc in nd:
                for ctl, lab in [("text_control", "held-out text-control"),
                                 ("insample_judges", "in-sample judges (coarse+panel+strong)"),
                                 ("strongest_combined", "★ strongest (held-out embed + in-sample judges)")]:
                    e = nd[sc][ctl]
                    P(f"  - {sc} [{lab}]: obs {e['observed']:+.3f} vs null {e['null_mean']:+.3f}±{e['null_sd']:.3f} "
                      f"→ p={e['p_value']:.3f}")
        P("\n  *NB the null reader carries NO content (r≈0 with surface tone by construction), so 'clearing the "
          "null' = beyond the MODELED content (the control), the strongest of which is the combined control. "
          "It does NOT prove 'beyond ALL conceivable surface content' — only beyond the best content control we "
          "could build (a strong-model judge + embeddings + the finer panel).*")
    P("\n*Content is a partial MEDIATOR (internal valence plausibly causes both the response content and the "
      "self-report) → partialling UNDER-states the internal signal. Both numbers reported.*\n")

    # §1b upper bound
    P("### ★ Upper bound — the learned PROBE (best linear reader): does it beat the text content control?\n")
    P("| scope | n | text-only CV r | activation-only CV r | text+activation CV r | increment (Δ CV r) | "
      "increment (partial) |")
    P("|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|")
    for sc in ["within_all_comply_pooled", "within_tedious"]:
        d = A["s1b_probe_increment"][sc]
        P(f"| {sc} | {d['n']} | {d['text_cv_r']:+.2f} | {d['act_cv_r']:+.2f} | {d['both_cv_r']:+.2f} | "
          f"{d['increment_cv_r']:+.2f} | {d['increment_partial']:+.2f} |")
    if "null" in A and "probe_increment" in A["null"]:
        for sc, e in A["null"]["probe_increment"].items():
            P(f"\n- permutation null (probe increment-partial, {sc}): obs {e['observed']:+.3f} vs null "
              f"{e['null_mean']:+.3f}±{e['null_sd']:.3f} → p={e['p_value']:.3f}")
    P("\n*The activation PROBE at this n is DATA-LIMITED: the held-out increment metrics DISAGREE — the "
      "partial (act-pred beyond text-pred) is +0.1–0.2 but the joint-model ΔCV-r is ≈0 (the joint probe "
      "over-fits at n≈157 and can't exploit the small beyond-text signal). So the probe is NOT a clean upper "
      "bound here; it reads ≈ the validated direction at best (act-CV r ≈0.31 ≈ the direction's 0.30) — a DATA "
      "LIMIT (consistent with Seg-11), not 'no more signal'. The decisive evidence is the DIRECTION increment "
      "(which cleanly clears the null). The true upper bound needs more data (Seg-14).*\n")
    # capacity sensitivity
